era_context: Continuation of the Iron Age II in the Levant and Eastern Mediterranean. Shalmaneser III's Assyrian campaigns remain the dominant military force in the region. The Kingdom of Israel continues under Jeroboam II or his predecessors, depending on chronological scheme. In Greece, the Archaic period progresses; early colonization movements are beginning. In China, the Western Zhou dynasty continues its decline.

events.title: Shalmaneser III's continued Levantine campaigns
events.description: Shalmaneser III continued his systematic campaigns into the Levant, maintaining Assyrian control over vassal states and extracting tribute. These annual campaigns were a hallmark of his reign and are documented in multiple Assyrian inscriptions. The campaigns reinforced Assyrian hegemony and prevented coalition-building among regional powers.
events.certainty_note: Shalmaneser III's campaigns are well-documented, but precise year-by-year assignment within his reign involves scholarly interpretation of inscription sequences.

disconfirming_evidence: The chronology of Shalmaneser III's reign and his individual campaigns remains subject to scholarly debate. Some scholars propose alternative dating schemes that would shift these events significantly. The 'low chronology' vs. 'high chronology' debate affects the absolute dating of all Iron Age Levantine events.
historiographic_note: For 828 BCE, sources are even more limited than for 829 BCE. Assyrian records provide the primary documentary evidence, but they are selective and propagandistic. No major events are clearly attested for this specific year in surviving sources; the entry above represents a continuation of documented patterns rather than a discrete, datable event. This illustrates the challenge of year-by-year history in the ancient world.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Shalmaneser III's campaign strategy
